title: Myosotis drucei
taxonomy: Myosotis drucei is classified within the family Boraginaceae. It is a member of the genus Myosotis, commonly known as forget-me-nots. This plant is native to the mountainous regions of China. The exact classification of Myosotis drucei is Myosotis drucei (Maxim.) Matsum. within the kingdom Plantae, division Magnoliophyta, class Magnoliopsida, order Boraginales, family Boraginaceae, genus Myosotis.

The plant is said to have anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which may contribute to its medicinal uses. intro: Myosotis drucei, commonly known as Druce's forget-me-not, is a perennial plant species belonging to the borage family (Boraginaceae). The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may aid in reducing inflammation and protecting against oxidative stress. The bioactive constituents of Myosotis drucei include flavonoids, such as quercetin and kaempferol, as well as alkaloids and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Myosotis drucei is used in traditional herbal medicine and is often prepared as a tea or infusion, as well as in the form of capsules or tinctures, to leverage its therapeutic benefits.
